turns.user_message: Someone made a quick joke at my expense yesterday, and it honestly used to roll right off my shoulders. I would have just laughed it off and moved on with my day without a second thought. But this time it just sat heavy in my chest all evening. I keep replaying the exact tone of their voice and wondering why I let it get under my skin so easily. It feels weird to be this rattled by something so small.

That same kind of offhand remark keeps echoing in my head, and I realize it happens way more often than I want to admit. I used to just brush past those moments, but lately I catch myself freezing up every time someone speaks to me that way. It makes me feel like I am constantly walking on eggshells just to avoid giving people another reason to sigh at me. I start questioning every little choice I make because I am terrified of being that annoying person again. The guilt just piles up until I feel completely drained.

I have started shrinking away from any interaction because I am convinced my mere presence is just another inconvenience for everyone around me. Those little comments used to bounce right off my back, but now they feel like absolute proof that I am fundamentally taking up too much space. I replay every single conversation looking for the exact moment I ruined the mood, and I hate that I cannot stop doing it. It is exhausting to carry this heavy sense of being a burden everywhere I go. I just wish I could figure out how to stop apologizing for existing in the first place.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
